title: peucedanum divaricatum
intro: Peucedanum divaricatum, also known as the forked or divaricate hog's fennel, is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family native to Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which are attributed to its essential oils and compounds. The health benefits of Peucedanum divaricatum include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and seeds are often dried and used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
